content:
The behaviour tree is a structured decision process that runs to completion on a unit of work (a message pair) and either produces an output or doesn't. It is not a continuous game AI tick — it's closer to the robotics application.

**Inputs:** a message pair (agent turn, user turn, repo path, timestamp, session context)
**Outputs:** one of — write to Octowiki, append to questions log, append to rejected directions, discard

**Invariants:**
- A pair is processed exactly once
- A failed classification never writes anything
- The tree never mutates Octowiki directly — it proposes a write, a separate write node executes it
- Every decision to write must carry provenance (session ID, timestamp, raw pair)

**Tree structure:**
```
Root (Sequence)
├── LoadProjectContext
│   ├── ReadOctowiki(relevant sections)
│   └── ReadRecentDecisions(last N entries)
├── PairAnalysis (Sequence)
│   ├── DetectRatification
│   ├── DetectDecision
│   ├── DetectRejection
│   ├── DetectOpenQuestion
│   └── DetectClarification
├── Selector: Act on strongest signal
│   ├── [Decision/Ratification] → ProposeWikiWrite
│   ├── [Rejection] → ProposeWikiWrite(rejected_directions)
│   ├── [OpenQuestion] → AppendToQuestions
│   ├── [Clarification] → UpdateProjectSummary
│   └── [no signal] → Discard
└── MarkProcessed
```

The root is a Sequence (not a Selector like shoe-makers) — all stages must run or abort.

provenance.raw_agent: The root is a Sequence, not a Selector. This is subtly different from shoe-makers.
provenance.raw_user: The tree structure: Root (Sequence) ├── LoadProjectContext... ├── PairAnalysis... ├── Selector: Act on strongest signal... └── MarkProcessed
